metadata.title:
JAIL NO TERROR TO THIS


PLUCLY GIRL SOCIALIST

pages.raw_text:
NEW YORK. Sept. ARREST
and Imprisonment over night in
the grimy tenderloin police sta
ton, charged with speaking on the
public streets without a license,
has not deferred the activity of 16
year old Elizabeth Flynn, a plucky
little socialist.


The girl has become famous as
public speaker in this city during
recent weeks. In a sweet, mellow
voice, full of dramatic force, she
has addressed thousands from rude
platforms or carts in the crowded
sections Of the city. Her father
Is a socialist. Two years ago he
suffered financial loss which re-
duced the family to comparative
poverty.


"If our laws had been just and
equal" said Miss Flynn, my
father would not have suffered as
he has. was only IA years old,
but clearly saw this injustice. |
made up my mind to study the
political conditions in this country.
| have and now my life will be de-
voted to the WORK of educating the
unthinking."


The girl Is a heroine on the East
Side. 1t was when she essayed
to speak on Broadway at night that
two men, In fashionable evening
clothes. coming from theater.
heard her and complained to the
police. They said she was talking
anarchy and the police locked her
up. The next day city magistrate
amazed at her flow of language
when she defended herself In his
court promptly discharged her.


Il shall go on" said Miss Flynn.
Prison bars do not Brighten when
one has truth and right deep in
the heart"
